document.title: TkVillage Migration Guide: declare_app
document.purpose: Guide Codex, Claude Code, and other LLM coding agents through migrating TkVillage applications from create_app(...) to declare_app(spec), including shutdown policy declarations.
author: Codex

summary.what-changed: TkVillage applications should now be declared with declare_app(spec), where spec is a single dictionary containing application identity, hosting/project-directory settings, runtime options, and a required shutdown policy.
summary.deprecated: create_app(...) is deprecated. It remains as a compatibility wrapper but should not be used in new or migrated code.
why: One dictionary keeps application declaration visible and inspectable.
The shutdown policy is now non-optional application identity.
Future declaration fields can be added without expanding positional function signatures.

important: Prefer lionscliapp mode for hosted apps. Use project-dir-name or project-dir only for standalone apps.
Call village.ensure_ready() at the top of lionscliapp commands that use TkVillage.
Use on-shutdown for the optional single shutdown handler.
Do not invent a second shutdown hook.

explicit.when-to-use: Use for apps that may keep running without visible windows, service-style TkVillage runtimes, tray-like apps, or test harnesses.
explicit.how-shutdown-starts: Application code calls village.request_shutdown(reason=None); shutdown begins at the end of a later tick.

on-last-window-close.when-to-use: Use for ordinary GUI apps where closing the final window should end the program.
on-last-window-close.how-shutdown-starts: After at least one window has existed, tick-end shutdown begins when rt.windows is empty.

on-window-close.when-to-use: Use when one declared window kind, usually the main window, owns program lifetime.
on-window-close.how-shutdown-starts: After a window of shutdown-window-kind has existed, tick-end shutdown begins when no live windows of that kind remain.

mechanical-steps-for-agents.instruction: Search for create_app(. Include direct imports from tkvillage.app and top-level tkvillage imports.

Replace each application startup call with declare_app({...}). Preserve the old name, tick interval, test mode, and create-root behavior as spec fields. For lionscliapp-hosted apps, use lionscliapp: True instead of a standalone project-dir-name.
For lionscliapp-hosted commands that use TkVillage paths or runtime, call village.ensure_ready() after lionscliapp has entered command runtime.
Choose explicit, on-last-window-close, or on-window-close based on application behavior. If unclear, prefer on-last-window-close for ordinary GUI apps and explicit for tests or service-like runtimes.
If the app has existing shutdown cleanup logic, put exactly one no-argument callable in on-shutdown. Otherwise use None.
Where app code wants graceful explicit shutdown, call village.request_shutdown(reason=None). Keep village.shutdown() only for hard immediate cleanup or tests.
If code imports create_app directly, change it to declare_app. Keep create_app only if intentionally testing the deprecated wrapper.
Run the existing tests. Add or update tests for the chosen shutdown policy.
Update local README or app docs to show declare_app(spec), not create_app(...).

notes: spec must include name and shutdown-policy.
standalone declarations must include project-dir-name or project-dir.
on-shutdown must be callable or None.
In lionscliapp mode, resolves lionscliapp's project root at command runtime and creates the tkvillage subdirectory.
In standalone mode, creates the declared project directory.
Does not stop the runtime immediately.
The actual shutdown begins at the end of tick_once().
It calls the configured shutdown handler once if it has not already run.
Do not use in migrated code.
It delegates to declare_app() with shutdown-policy explicit and no shutdown handler.

verification-checklist: No ordinary app startup path calls create_app(...).
lionscliapp-hosted commands call ensure_ready() before using TkVillage project files.
Standalone declarations include project-dir-name or project-dir.
At most one on-shutdown handler is declared.
Explicit shutdown flows call request_shutdown(), not shutdown(), unless immediate hard cleanup is intended.
Tests cover the intended shutdown policy.
